title: Sales & Account Growth Manager — SaaS for Fashion Industry (Remote)
description:
About the role

We are a SaaS platform that helps fashion brands manage their product development (tech packs, purchase orders, shipments, supplier communication). Think of it as the operating system for apparel brands.



I'm looking for someone who will own revenue growth. That means running demos to close new business, onboarding the clients you sell, and then growing those accounts over time. You're not a support person who occasionally sells — you're a salesperson who makes sure your clients succeed so they stick around and expand.



What you'll actually be doing

Prospecting and outreach to fashion brands who'd benefit from the platform (mostly calls will be booked via ads for you). Running live demos via Google Meet, handling objections, and closing deals. Managing
 communication with prospects and clients throughout the sales cycle and beyond — you'll need to stay on top of threads, follow up promptly, and keep conversations moving as deal cycles can take 14-30 days. Onboarding new clients you've sold — training them on the platform so they get value fast. Managing ongoing client relationships to reduce churn and identify upsell opportunities. Keeping the CRM updated with pipeline activity, deal stages, and client notes — if it's not in the CRM, it didn't happen. You'll be trained on the product — you don't need to know it already. But you do need to be someone who takes ownership of a number and finds ways to hit it.



Requirements

Native or near-native American English is non-negotiable. You'll be on calls with US-based brand owners and need to communicate clearly and confidently. Proven track record in sales — you've carried a quota before and can talk about how you hit it. Strong written communication — a big part of this role is
. Experience with CRM tools (HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive, or similar). Self-starter who doesn't need to be micromanaged. Available during US business hours.

Compensation



$1,000–$1,500 USD/month base depending on experience, plus commission on every deal you close. This is a ground-floor role at a growing startup — the better you sell, the more you make, and the role grows with the company.



To apply

Include a short voice recording (60–90 seconds) introducing yourself and telling me why you'd be a good fit. Applications without a recording won't be considered. I'm looking to fill this within 48 hours! :)

title: Link Acquisition & Digital PR Specialist (Remote)
description:
About the Role 

We are Penji (penji.co), an on-demand design service. We don't need low-quality directory links or spammy blog comments. We are looking for a creative, resilient outreach specialist who can get our brand featured on high-authority design, marketing, and business publications.

You will not just be "building links." You will be building relationships and acting as a PR bridge between our content and the rest of the internet.



The Mission

• Hunt High-Value Targets: Identify relevant websites, blogs, and journalists in the design/tech/SaaS space that actually matter.

• Craft "Un-Ignoreable"
s: Write personalized cold outreach
s that get opened and replied to. No copy-paste templates that sound like a bot.

• Leverage Our Design Power: You have a team of professional designers at your disposal. Use them! Pitch infographics, visual stats, and guest posts that look amazing to secure placements.

• Negotiate & Close: Manage the back-and-forth communication to secure the backlink, whether through guest posting, broken link building, or digital PR campaigns.

• Analyze & Report: Track your wins. We care about the Domain Rating (DR) and Traffic of the links you get, not just the total number.



Who You Are

• You are a Sniper, not a Spammer: You’d rather get 3 links from reputable sites (DR 60+) than 50 links from junk sites.

• You speak "Fluent Internet": Your written English is impeccable. You know how to be persuasive without sounding salesy.

• You are Thick-Skinned: You handle rejection well and know that a "No" is just part of the process.

• Tool Proficient: You are an expert in Ahrefs or Semrush. You know how to analyze a competitor’s backlink profile and steal their strategy.



Requirements

• Proven Portfolio: Success in Off-Page SEO with a portfolio of high-DR links you’ve personally secured.

• Technical SEO Mastery: Strong understanding of Google’s Link Quality Guidelines and the ability to differentiate between a real site and a "Link Farm."

• Competitive Strategy: Experience with Backlink Gap Analysis and studying competitor profiles via Ahrefs or Semrush.

• Outreach Expert: Proficiency with tools like Pitchbox, BuzzStream, or 
.

• White Hat Purist: We strictly follow White Hat practices to protect our long-term domain health.

• Quality Control: Ability to evaluate website relevance and "true" authority beyond just a DR score.



How to Apply

To ensure your application stands out:

1. Complete the application form:

2 . Attach your portfolio with the following details: 

• Target URL

• Source URL

• Domain Rating (DR)

• Link Type (guest post, niche edit, resource link, etc.)

• Anchor text used

• Dofollow / Nofollow status

• Paid / Free backlink

We work with ambitious people who want real, lasting change — and our marketing needs to reflect that energy.

What you'll do - things like:

 list with campaigns that convert

You're a great fit if you…

The role could start as full-time or grow from part-time to full-time position in the future, or we can add other virtual tea
mbers if you prefer to stay part-time.

This role requires excellent oral and written English communication skills, high attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. The ideal candidate is proactive, organised, and capable of working both independently and as part of a team.

title: Create high quality AI videos
description:
Klassieke Arresten in Beeld ("Classic Case Law in Pictures") is a Dutch legal media initiative that turns landmark Dutch court rulings into short, cinematic AI-generated videos. The project was founded by three Dutch legal professionals and launched on LinkedIn in late 2025. Our first videos, on the Kelderluik and Porsche cases, generated hundreds of enthusiastic reactions from law students, lawyers, and academics across the Netherlands.



The mission is simple: make classic case law more accessible and engaging for the next generation of lawyers, while showcasing how AI is reshaping legal education and media. We publish a new video every two weeks on LinkedIn, with YouTube and TikTok channels rolling out next.



The bottleneck right now is production time. We build every video ourselves from scratch, including detailed art boards in Nano Banana for every shot. The creative direction is sharp, but the manual work is slowing us down. That is why we are hiring.



The role

We are looking for a serious talent in AI video production to take ownership of the technical base of each video, so our team can focus on creative direction, storytelling, and final polish. You will be the production engine of the project: building first versions of videos at a steady pace, on a clear weekly rhythm.



This is a part-time, fully remote position. We are open to talent from anywhere in the world. What matters to us is craftsmanship, reliability, and a real obsession with what AI tools can do. We are looking for a long-term collaboration, not a one-off project.



What you will do

You will produce the foundational version of each video based on a brief and script we provide. Concretely, this means:



Building detailed art boards in Nano Banana for every scene in the video, in line with our visual style guide. Generating video shots in Google Veo 3, and experimenting with newer models such as Seedance whenever they outperform the current setup. We continuously test and switch to whatever delivers the best results. Generating Dutch voice-overs in ElevenLabs using the voices we provide. Sourcing or generating music in Suno that fits the tone of the case. Assembling a complete first cut in CapCut, including timing, transitions, on-screen text, and basic sound design. Delivering a clean, structured project file that our team can refine and finalize.



You will not need to write scripts, pick which cases we cover, or handle publishing. That stays with our team. Your job is to take a finished script and brief, and turn it into a polished first version we can perfect.



Working language and final output

All briefs, scripts, and instructions are delivered in both Dutch and English. We will always make crystal clear which text belongs where, what should appear on screen, and what needs to be voiced. The final videos themselves are produced in Dutch, but you do not need to speak or understand Dutch yourself. Our team handles all language nuance and final review.



What we are looking for

The single most important thing: you can build stunning art boards and video sequences with AI, and you can prove it. Tools come and go, but the eye for composition, consistency, and storytelling is what we are hiring for.



Beyond that:

Hands-on experience with Nano Banana or comparable image generation tools for art board production. Proven experience with AI video generation, ideally Google Veo 3, Sora, Seedance, or comparable models. A strong portfolio of AI-generated video work, even if from personal projects. Solid editing skills in CapCut or comparable software. Experience with ElevenLabs and Suno, or the willingness to master them quickly. A sharp eye for visual consistency, pacing, and storytelling. Strong written and spoken English. The discipline to deliver on deadlines without close supervision. Bonus: any background or interest in law, history, or educational content.



What we offer

A long-term, stable part-time role with a fast-growing Dutch legal media company. A creative project with real impact in the legal community, and visible work that builds your portfolio. Direct collaboration with the founders, with clear briefs and quick feedback loops.



Workload and rhythm

We aim for one video every two weeks at minimum, scaling up to weekly as the project grows. Estimated workload: 15 to 25 hours per week to start, with potential to expand. You set your own hours, as long as deliverables meet our deadlines.



How to apply

A short introduction about yourself and why this project interests you. Your portfolio or links to AI-generated video work you have produced. A brief description of the AI tools you use most often and your level of experience with each.



As part of the selection process, shortlisted candidates will be asked to produce a short paid test video based on a brief we provide. This gives both of us a clear sense of fit before we commit.



We review applications on a rolling basis and aim to onboard our new specialist within two weeks.

title: Marketing Coordinator – Freelance | Australian Education Start-Up
description:

title: Bookkeeper (QuickBooks | AU Clients)
description:
We seek a detailed Accounting professional with Australian-client experience to work within precise rules and procedures to maintain very high standards of accuracy within a growing business environment.



ABOUT THE ROLE

Kalibrate-Ed is a growing, education strategy organisation working with Australian clients, contractors, and partners. We’re looking for a Bookkeeper who can meticulously manage day-to-day financial operations to keep our numbers accurate and up-to-date.



This is a flexible, remote role with permanent ongoing work in a friendly team environment with clear deadlines and steady outcomes.



WHAT YOU'LL DO

• Work within precise rules and procedures to maintain very high standards of accuracy within a team environment.

• Keep the company’s financial books and transactions organised, up-to-date and compliant.

• Actively identify and report variances, errors and anomalies in financial figures.

• Process accounts receivable, including client income arrangements and payment verification.

• Correctly categorise and process expense transactions, including correct tax codes and ABR verifications.

• Actively manage clear financial deadlines and prepare end-of-month reconciliation and ATO submissions – including IAS, BAS and STP.

• Analyse financial data and create leadership reports and forecasting.

• Create and manage compliance with company’s financial policies and SOP.



YOU MUST HAVE:

• Qualifications as an accountant (CPA, CA) working with QuickBooks

• Minimum 3+ years of demonstrable experience in accounting in a remote contractor role

• Experience working with Australian clients within their local taxation and payroll systems

• Expertise in QuickBooks with proficiency in MSOffice 365



YOU WILL POSSESS:

• Professional with expertise in accounting and finance. This role is not suited to a general VA.

• Clear communication skills to coordinate activity and resolve discrepancies with internal and external stakeholders.

• Meticulous attention to detail - able to spot mistakes easily.

• Proactive organise for deadlines with the flexibility Adapt and Respond – to time-sensitive ad-hoc items as they arise and effectively reorganise to hit deadlines.



WORKING ARRANGEMENTS

• Paid on a fixed monthly, project-based fee

• This is a flexible work arrangement; however, there may be urgent items such as New Client payment set up or ad-hoc tasks to resolve finance issues, you are expected to actively monitor communication during AU business hours and prioritise accordingly.



TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

• Expert knowledge of QuickBooks; strong working knowledge of Microsoft 365 Suite (Teams, Excel, Outlook, SharePoint).

• Home-based, quiet and professional workspace.

• High-speed internet (>100mbps) with power with connectivity and power back-up.

• PC laptop (i5 or higher), with dual screens and headset preferred.



HOW TO APPLY

To be considered for this role,
 your complete application package to 



• Your CV – detailing your QuickBooks and AU experience, please also include two contactable references.

• Cover letter with:



a. Short summary of your experience.

b. Responses to these questions:



• Please state 3-5 Australian-taxation specific requirements or processes you’ve been responsible for.

• Please outline the steps or stages you’d take to ensure accurate and on-time submission of BAS in Australia?

• What specific tasks do you expect to be doing day-to-day?

• What do you expect will be the biggest challenge in the role?

• What is your process for ensuring meticulous accuracy of all financial numbers?



Kindly also include answers to the following Screening Questions within your application:

• Do you have 3+ years' experience using QuickBooks?

• Do you have experience working with Australian clients?

• Do you have experience preparing BAS or IAS in the Australian Taxation system?

• Do you understand what it means to work as an independent contractor or freelancer with ongoing work (as opposed to an employee)?

• Do you have a licensed MS Office desktop app installed?

• Are you a qualified accountant (CPA, CA)? Please state your qualifications.

title: Influencer Operations Specialist

title: AI Implementation, Training, & Automation Specialist
description:
Position: AI Implementation & Automation Specialist

Location: Remote 

Type: Contract / Project-Based (with potential for ongoing work)



About the Role



We are seeking a highly capable AI Implementation Specialist to support business advisory projects focused on improving performance, efficiency, and growth.



This is not a theoretical or research role.



This role is focused on practical application of AI tools to real business environments — helping companies streamline operations, improve decision-making, and create measurable value.



You will work alongside a senior business advisor, supporting leadership teams across a range of industries.



What You’ll Be Doing



• Assess current business workflows and identify opportunities for AI integration

• Recommend and implement practical AI tools (automation, workflows, reporting, communication)

• Build and deploy systems using tools such as ChatGPT, Zapier, Make, CRMs, and other AI platforms

• Simplify and automate repetitive processes across operations, sales, and internal systems

• Help leadership teams understand how to use AI effectively (simple, practical training)

• Translate business needs into actionable AI solutions (not overcomplicated systems)

• Continuously improve systems based on real-world usage and feedback



What We’re Looking For



• Proven experience implementing AI tools in real business environments

• Strong understanding of business workflows (operations, sales, admin, etc.)

• Experience with automation tools (Zapier, Make, APIs, CRM integrations, etc.)

• Ability to simplify — not overcomplicate

• Strong English communication skills  and you can explain things clearly to non-technical people

• Self-directed and proactive — able to take ownership of projects

• Comfortable working alongside leadership teams



Bonus Experience



• Experience working with SMBs ($1M–$20M revenue range)

• CRM implementation (HubSpot, Salesforce, etc.)

• Process optimization / operations consulting

• Experience building dashboards, reporting systems, or internal tools



How We Work



We focus on:



• practical solutions, not theory

• speed and clarity over complexity

• real business outcomes (efficiency, growth, cost savings)



Compensation



• Project-based or monthly retainer (based on experience and scope)

• Opportunity for ongoing work across multiple client engagements

title: Back-End Developer for Fast-Growing AI SaaS Platform
description:
We are a fast-growing SaaS company launching an AI-powered platform that combines CRM, AI content generation, lead generation, social media automation, messaging infrastructure, and advanced business tools into one unified system. We are preparing for launch and need skilled back-end developers to join our engineering team under our lead engineer.

This is a long-term, full-time opportunity for the right person. We are hiring up to three back-end developers who will work directly under our lead engineer to build, test, and maintain features on our platform.



What You'll Be Doing:



Building and maintaining back-end functions, integrations, and automation workflows

Working with REST APIs, database queries, and third-party integrations (GoHighLevel, OpenAI, Stripe, and others)

Writing clean, efficient code that handles real user load at scale

Troubleshooting, debugging, and optimizing existing systems

Collaborating with our lead engineer and following established architecture patterns

Participating in code reviews and team standups



What We're Looking For:

We are NOT looking for senior-level rock stars who charge premium rates. We are looking for developers with the following qualities:



Trainable — You are open to learning our stack and our way of doing things

Adaptable — You can pivot when priorities shift or new technologies are introduced

Strong character — You are honest, reliable, communicate clearly, and take ownership of your work

Problem solver — You can take a problem, think it through, and propose a solution (more important than knowing every framework)

Reliable communicator — You respond to messages, show up for meetings, and give honest status updates

English proficiency — Strong written English for Slack and documentation



Experience level: Mid-level is ideal. Junior developers with strong fundamentals and a great attitude will be considered.



Technical Skills (nice to have, not all required):



JavaScript / TypeScript

REST API development and integration

Working with cloud platforms (AWS, DigitalOcean, or similar)

Database experience (PostgreSQL, MySQL, or similar)

Experience with no-code/low-code platforms is a bonus

Familiarity with AI/LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) is a plus





What We Offer:



Long-term, stable work with a growing company

Opportunity to grow — as we scale, your role and compensation will scale with us

Work directly with our lead engineer who manages our development team

Flexible hours — we care about output, not clock-watching

Remote position — work from home

Full-time preferred (40+ hours per week)





Hiring Process:



Initial application — submit your application with the information requested below

Paid test project — shortlisted candidates will be given a small paid project to complete. We pay you for this even if we don't move forward with hiring. The test is designed to evaluate how you approach problems and communicate your thinking, not just whether you get the right answer.

Conversation with our lead engineer and founders — if your test goes well, we'll have a short call to get to know you

Offer — if we're aligned, we'll make you an offer and get you started





How to Apply:

Please send your application with the following:



Your full name and location

A brief introduction (3–5 sentences) about who you are

Years of back-end development experience

Technical skills and tools you're comfortable with

A link to your GitHub, portfolio, or any recent work you're proud of (if available)

Your current work situation (are you working elsewhere, how many hours per week are you available)

Your desired hourly rate (please be realistic — we value long-term relationships over one-time high rates)

Answer this question in 2–3 sentences: "Describe a problem you solved recently that you're proud of. What made the solution work?"



Applications that skip step 8 will not be considered. We want to see how you think.



Important:



We move fast but we hire carefully. Please be patient with our process.

We are looking for people who want to be part of something growing, not just collect a paycheck.

If you are juggling multiple clients and can't commit real hours, this is not the right role for you right now.



We look forward to hearing from you...
salary: Starting rate based on experience and pa

title: Online Math/Science Teacher(Near-Native Accent)
description:
About Pomelo Academy

Pomelo Academy is an innovative online international school dedicated to delivering high-quality education to students worldwide. We provide a dynamic and engaging learning environment, catering to individualized instruction that transcends geographical boundaries.



Job Description

We are seeking passionate and experienced Near-Native English-speaking Online K-12 Math and/or Science Teachers with verifiable teaching certifications. The ideal candidates will have strong academic backgrounds in Mathematics and/or Science, experience teaching young learners (ages 5–18), and a commitment to creating a supportive, inquiry-based, and engaging online learning environment.



Responsibilities



Deliver interactive and engaging Math and/or Science lessons to individuals and small groups of 2–6 students aged 5–18.

Teach using internationally recognized curricula such as A Closer Look, Glencoe Science, Pearson Interactive Science, NGSS-aligned resources, Singapore Math, or IB/AP/A Level Math programs.

Create age-appropriate lesson plans focused on conceptual understanding, inquiry, problem-solving, and real-world applications.

Incorporate visuals, multimedia, and hands-on virtual activities to make abstract concepts accessible and engaging.

Monitor student progress and provide timely feedback to students and parents.

Foster curiosity, discussion, and active learning in a safe and supportive space.



Qualifications



Professional teacher with at least 2 years of teaching experience (Kindergarten–Grade 12).

Must hold a valid teaching license/certification (e.g., Teaching License, State Certification). TESOL/TEFL is a plus.

Near-native English accent with excellent spoken and written communication skills.

Strong academic background in Mathematics and/or Science (Biology, Physics, Chemistry, General Science).

Experience teaching in public/international schools or reputable online platforms.

Familiarity with inquiry-based and/or interdisciplinary approaches (e.g., STEM/STEAM).

Comfortable working between 07:00–22:00 (Weekdays) and 9:00–22:00 (Weekends), GMT+8.

Proficiency with digital platforms such as ClassIn, Microsoft Office, and other educational technologies.

Must have a stable and fast internet connection and a PC/laptop with at least Windows 10 or Mac OS Catalina.



Benefits



Flexible working hours.

Opportunity to teach using globally respected Math and Science curricula.

Be part of an international, passionate team of educators.

Access to training and professional development.

Regular enrollment model (not a booking system), ensuring schedule stability and consistency

title: Property Management Support
description:
Job Description - Property Management Support



We are looking for someone to join the Moorview Lets team in the Property Management Support role. This role will work closely with the Property Managers within the business, to deliver 5* service to both tenants and landlords.



PLEASE NOTE WE WILL ONLY CONSIDER APPLICANTS WHO HAVE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT EXPERIENCE, SPECIFICALLY DEALING WITH MAINTENANCE TASKS.



The ideal candidate will be highly organised, detail-orientated, with an excellent
 manner and high standards for their work.

The ideal candidate should be proficient at using IT and be able to use tools like MS Office and the Google suite of products.



Responsibilities

* Work daily through your assigned tasks to move them forward, close them out, or get more information from tenants

* Liaise with tenants, landlords and contractors to efficiently triage/diagnose and resolve maintenance tasks

* Switch easily between planned and reactive maintenance items

* Take ownership for a portfolio of properties

* Keep excellent records on our property management system 'COHO'

* Update core KPIs with commentary 

* Approve invoices for works and send these for payment

* Work closely with other parts of the business (e.g. onboarding, sales and finance)

* Other administrative duties as required by the Property Managers



Requirements

* Property management experience (specifically maintenance management) is essential

* Proficiency in MS Office (MS Excel and MS Powerpoint in particular) and using Google Suite

* Excellent time management skills and ability to prioritise work

* Attention to detail and problem solving skills

* Excellent written and verbal communication skills

* Strong organisational skills and ability to multi-task

* Ability to triage and prioritise maintenance tasks

* Flexible with a 'can do' attitude and growth mindset, prepared to take on new challenges.



What we can offer you

As a company we believe in creating a working environment where people feel cared for, safe and have opportunities to develop their skills. We welcome open and honest communication in the business to enable transparent understanding of the working environment. Collaboratively we support each other to improve and in doing so we encourage a strong challenge and support model in day to day tasks and events to accelerate personal development and business growth.

To this end we have an open working style which is friendly and fun but also professional. Alongside our commitment to keep the team healthy and safe, we also believe passionately in providing opportunities within the company for people to develop their skills and knowledge.



Working Pattern: Full time, Mon-Friday, 9am-5pm UK time (with an hour for lunch)



Holidays: 15 days per year + UK Bank Holidays

title: Solar Proposal & Design Specialist
description:
Company: SunSource Power LLC

Location: Honolulu, HI (Remote / Virtual)

Employment Type: Full-Time



We are seeking a Solar Proposal & Design Specialist to join our growing team. This role is ideal for a detail-oriented, technically skilled professional with strong experience in solar design, AutoCAD drafting, and permitting. You will be responsible for creating accurate solar proposals, producing permit-ready designs, and managing permitting submissions across multiple counties in Hawaii. This role serves as a critical link between sales and installation, ensuring projects move efficiently from design through approval.



Key Responsibilities



Proposal Generation

- Create accurate, customer-ready solar proposals using Aurora, Helioscope, or similar design software

- Interpret Hawaii-specific utility rates, incentives, and programs (NEM Plus, CGS+, Smart DER Export)

- Analyze site conditions using satellite imagery and site survey data to properly size PV systems

- Collaborate with the sales team to deliver tailored proposals aligned with customer needs



Design & AutoCAD

- Produce detailed PV system layouts, conduit routing, and equipment placement using AutoCAD

- Develop permit-ready drawing sets including roof plans, electrical diagrams, and schematics

- Ensure all designs comply with Hawaii Building Code, NEC standards, and utility interconnection requirements

- Coordinate with engineers and AHJs to address corrections and design revisions



Permitting & Documentation

- Prepare and submit permit applications to county permitting offices across Hawaii (Honolulu, Maui, Kauai, Hawaii County)

- Track permit timelines, respond to correction notices, and manage approvals through issuance

- Maintain updated records of jurisdictional requirements and permitting processes



Required Skills & Qualifications (Non-Negotiable)

1. Proven experience in solar system design

2. Proficiency in AutoCAD

3. Solar permitting experience (If you have it great, if not, we will train you)

4. Ability to redline designs and complete accurate as-built drawings



Preferred Skills

1. Knowledge of Hawaii’s solar incentive programs and utility interconnection processes

2. Experience preparing permit-ready drawing sets and managing permitting submissions

3. Previous experience working in the Hawaii solar market





Compensation & Benefits

- Full-time position with competitive pay (based on experience)

- Flexible, remote/virtual work environment

- Opportunity to grow within a mission-driven solar energy company

- Paid federal holidays



How to Apply



To apply, complete your application by submitting it through the form at the link below.



 Application Form: 



Only applications that are fully completed through  the application form will be considered.

title: Elite Video Editor (Fitness & Anime Branding)
description:
About the Channel



Gokuflex is an 11-year-old fitness brand that blends heavy lifting and fatherhood with Dragon Ball Z mythology. We build Super Saiyans in real life.



We are not looking for a basic "button pusher" who just slaps transitions on gym footage. We are looking for a cinematic storyteller who understands how to build tension, utilize sound design, and make a standard training vlog feel like an anime boss battle.



What You Will Be Editing



Cinematic YouTube Vlogs (Training, lifestyle, and mindset)



Client Transformation Spotlights (High-emotion, high-retention stories)



YouTube Shorts / Instagram Reels (Viral pacing, aggressive hooks)



The Non-Negotiables (Do not apply if you lack these)



Deep DBZ Knowledge: You need to know the arcs, the lore, and the music. If you don't know what a Senzu Bean or the Time Chamber is, you will fail here.



Real Fitness Experience: You must understand gym culture, lifting mechanics, and body transformations.



No Lazy Editing: We do not use cheap stock footage or generic templates. You must know how to color grade, mix audio, and build custom tension.



Software: Professional mastery of Premiere Pro, Final Cut Pro, or DaVinci Resolve.



Work Structure



100% remote, work from home.



Consistent weekly video schedule with strict 48 to 72-hour turnaround deadlines.



Paid in USD via Wise. Starting at $800/month scaling to $1,200 based on experience and performance.



How to Apply

Attention to detail is our #1 core value. If you cannot follow these exact instructions, your application will be deleted.



Send your application with the subject line: SAIYAN EDITOR - [Your Name]



In the very first line of your
, you must type this exact sentence: "Hey Matt, I am ready for the Time Chamber."



Include links to 2 or 3 of your absolute best fitness or cinematic edits.



Answer the required question below.



REQUIRED TEST QUESTION:



Vegeta’s pride is his greatest strength, but it also causes his biggest failures. If you were editing a transformation video about a client who was stuck at a plateau for years because his ego wouldn't let him drop the weight and fix his form, how would you visually and audibly edit that narrative? Give specific examples of pacing, sound design, and DBZ integration.

title: Fast, Accurate eCommerce Product Lister (AI-Assisted Workflow)
description:
We are a rapidly growing, US-based online bookstore specializing in high-value, rare, and academic non-fiction. We are looking for a highly organized, fast, and detail-oriented Virtual Assistant to join our team as an eCommerce Product Lister.



The Role:

You will be responsible for creating high-quality product drafts on eBay. You do NOT need to write descriptions or determine pricing from scratch. We have a highly streamlined, AI-assisted workflow. We will provide you with:



A Master Google Sheet for tracking



Links to professional 4K product photos



Access to a premium AI tool with our exact step-by-step prompts



Your job is to follow our Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) to:



Feed product photos into the AI tool to generate accurate Condition Reports, HTML descriptions, and optimized titles.



Conduct quick market research on eBay (Active vs. Sold) to determine pricing based on our guidelines.



Build the draft securely in our eBay Team account using "Sell Similar."



Upload photos, paste the HTML, fill in accurate Item Specifics (like Publication Year, ISBN), and update the Master Sheet.



Requirements:



Speed & Accuracy: You must be able to comfortably draft 3 to 5 listings per hour once you learn the system.



Tech-Savvy: Comfortable using AI tools, Google Sheets, Google Drive, and navigating eCommerce backend dashboards.



Detail-Oriented: A single typo in a price or ISBN can cause major issues, so attention to detail is your most important skill.



Reliable Internet: You will be downloading and uploading multiple image files per listing.



Communication: Upper-intermediate to Advanced English reading and writing skills.



Work Hours & Compensation:



Part-Time (approx. 30 hours per week / 6 hours per day).



Rate: $1.70 - $2.00 / hour (Depending on experience and speed).



There is high potential to transition to full-time as we expand our inventory.



How to Apply:

To prove you have read this entire job description and possess the attention to detail we require, you MUST start your application message with the phrase: "I am ready to build the Tree of Knowledge." Applications without this exact phrase will be automatically deleted. Please also include a brief sentence about your experience using AI tools (like ChatGPT or Gemini) or general eCommerce data entry.

title: Junior Back-End Developer — Long-Term Remote Role
description:
About the Role:

We are a growing SaaS company preparing to launch an AI-powered platform. We are hiring junior back-end developers to join our engineering team under an experienced lead developer.

This is a long-term, remote, full-time opportunity for someone who wants to grow with a company from the ground up.



What We're Looking For:



Basic back-end development skills (APIs, databases, server-side logic)

Willingness to learn and take direction from our lead engineer

Reliable and consistent — you show up, communicate clearly, and finish what you start

Good written English

We value Good Character, Adaptable and Trainable over skillset and expertise.  

Full-time availability (40+ hours per week) 



Experience level: Junior is fine. We care more about attitude, reliability, and problem-solving than how many years you've coded.



Pay:

$4-$6/hour starting rate, based on experience and test performance. Increases available as you prove yourself.



How to Apply:

Send a short message with:



1. Your name and location

2. Years of back-end development experience

3. Tools and languages you're comfortable with

4. Are you currently working elsewhere? How many hours per week are you available?

5. Answer this in 2–3 sentences: "Describe a coding problem you solved recently and how you approached it."



Applications missing question #5 will be ignored.



Next Steps:

If we like your application, we'll send you a small paid test project. If that goes well, we'll have a short call. Then an offer.

We move fast. If you're serious, apply today.

title: Senior UX Specialist
description:
About Us

2DC is an Australian digital agency with offices in Australia, the Philippines and the UK. We deliver large-scale web and mobile platforms for enterprise clients across government, health, membership organisations and the not-for-profit sector.





About the Role

2DC is growing. We have several large-scale platform projects running concurrently and we’re expanding our team to meet demand. This role is part of that expansion.



We work to a four-stage delivery model across our major projects – Discovery, Define, Design, and Develop – and depending on project timing, you may be brought into engagements at different stages. What’s consistent across all of them is the need for strong, senior UX thinking from the 
nt you’re in.



The work is complex. Our clients typically run large, content-heavy platforms with multiple user segments, tiered access models, and distinct logged-in and logged-out states that need careful, considered design. Think national vs. state structures, member tiers, different audience types with different levels of access – the kind of IA challenges that require real experience to navigate confidently.



The core of what you’ll be doing across these projects:

- Leading information architecture and sitemap design for large, content-heavy platforms

- Designing and articulating logged-in vs. logged-out states across different user types and access levels

- Translating substantial bodies of research into clear structural decisions and wireframes

- Working through migration logic in collaboration with our specialists – what stays, what gets restructured, what gets retired

- Considering mobile app ecosystems alongside website work where relevant



You’ll be expected to hit the ground running. These aren’t projects where everything is handed to you – they require someone who can synthesise complexity, make confident UX calls, and clearly articulate the rationale behind every structural decision.



This role is not suitable for junior or mid-level UX practitioners. The scope is large, the subject matter is complex, and the decision-making load is high.



---

What You Will Be Doing



Discovery and Research Synthesis

- Analysing existing site behaviour using Hotjar or equivalent tools – reviewing heatmaps, scroll maps, session recordings and click data, and producing clear outputs that communicate how users are currently navigating the site

- Distilling workshop outputs, interview findings and behavioural data into a structured view of user needs, pains and opportunities, prioritised by impact

- Identifying gaps in existing research and recommending additional qualitative or quantitative methods where needed



Information Architecture and UX Strategy

- Designing a clear, considered IA and sitemap that accounts for multiple user segments, access tiers, and complex content governance

- Creating user personas, journey maps and interaction models that guide structural and content decisions

- Mapping primary and secondary user journeys across web and app touchpoints, identifying friction points, drop-off risks and 
nts of value



Wireframing and Handoff

- Building wireframes and interactive prototypes in Figma to communicate structure and intent to developers and stakeholders

- Producing clear, developer-ready UX specifications the build team can work from directly

- Supporting usability review throughout build and QA phases



Collaboration

- Working closely with art directors, content specialists, developers and project managers across Australian and international time zones

- Presenting findings and structural recommendations to senior stakeholders with confidence and clarity





What We Are Looking For

- At least 10 years of hands-on UX experience across complex digital products, ideally spanning both web and native mobile

- Experience with content-heavy platforms requiring complex information architecture – this is a critical requirement for this role

- Demonstrated ability to conduct and synthesise behavioural analytics – Hotjar or equivalent – and present findings clearly

- Strong working knowledge of WCAG accessibility standards and inclusive design principles

- Expert-level Figma proficiency – you need to be genuinely fast in Figma, not just competent. The timeline is tight and the - volume of deliverables is high; slow Figma work is a blocker

- Confident communication skills, comfortable presenting to senior stakeholders

- Self-motivated and reliable, able to work independently in a remote environment while staying closely connected to the wider team

- A portfolio that demonstrates your end-to-end UX process – research, synthesis, strategy, and wireframing – not just final visual outputs





Nice to Have

- Experience with AI-assisted design tools such as Figma AI

- Familiarity with GA4 or similar analytics platforms

- Previous work with Australian clients or within Australian digital agencies





What We Offer

- AUD $20 per hour for the right person

- Full-time contract, work from home

- Day shift hours: 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM Philippine time on scheduled working days

- Genuinely interesting, large-scale projects with a senior and collaborative delivery team





How to Apply

Submit your CV, portfolio, and a brief cover note explaining why this role is the right fit for you. Your portfolio should include at least one example that shows your research and synthesis process – not just the end result.

If shortlisted, the next stage will include a practical skills assessment.

title: Amazon PPC/Ads Manager (Remote)

title: Digital Marketing Specialist (Google Ads and Social Media)
description:

, Instagram, TikTok, Linkedin).

title: Client Communications & Operations VA — Golden Stain (Home Services | Colorado, USA)
description:
Golden Stain is a growing home services company based in Colorado specializing in fence and deck staining, deck rebuilds, and interior remodeling. We move fast, we hold high standards, and we are building something real. This is not a placeholder job. The right person will grow with this company and eventually oversee full operations as we expand into a second business.



This is a full-time remote position for someone who is sharp, ambitious, and takes ownership. You will be the backbone of our client-facing operations and the person keeping everything running while the owner focuses on growth. We are heading into busy season, and the first week will require heavy lifting to get caught up. If that excites you, you are probably the right person.



This role is not for someone who wants to be told what to do every hour. It is for someone who sees a problem, solves it, and reports back. We do not want a yes-person. We want someone business-minded who will push back when something can be done better.



WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING:



Managing all client communications via text,
, and phone on behalf of the owner. Scheduling estimates and jobs using Housecall Pro. Creating estimates based on owner guidelines. Managing and responding to our Google Business Profile. Overseeing social media scheduling and coordination with our content editor. Supervising our intern team and keeping tasks moving. Sending the owner a daily Slack update covering what happened, what is pending, and what needs attention. Keeping operations tight and flagging issues before they escalate.



WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:



Excellent written and spoken English with client-facing communication experience. Experience with CRM or scheduling tools, like Housecall Pro, is a big plus. An ambitious ownership-driven mindset with a desire to grow and eventually lead. Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple priorities at once. Comfortable and confident making calls to clients on behalf of the owner. I am available to work Colorado business hours in Mountain Time, six days a week, with the possibility of occasional 5-day weeks depending on the season.



BONUS SKILLS WE WANT TO KNOW ABOUT:



In your application, tell us your experience level with each of the following. These are not required but the right person will have room to take these on and be compensated for it.

WordPress website updates and basic edits. Local SEO and Google ranking. Meta Ads campaign management. Any other marketing, business, or tech skills you bring to the table.



EQUIPMENT REQUIREMENTS:



You must have your own reliable high-speed internet, a quality microphone, and a capable computer or laptop. These are non-negotiable.



COMPENSATION AND GROWTH:



Starting pay is $650 per month. After 30 days, a performance review will be conducted, with the potential to earn more based on results. A bonus is included for any week that exceeds 50 hours to make sure nothing gets missed. Long-term opportunity to oversee full operations and eventually manage a team as the company scales into new ventures.



HOW TO APPLY:



Send your resume along with a short voice or video recording that covers three things. First, introduce yourself and walk us through your relevant experience. Second, tell us your experience level with each of the bonus skills listed above. Third, tell us why you are the right fit and what makes you different. Applications without a recording will not be reviewed.

title: Executive Assistant to the Founder
description:
Forget What You Think an EA Does.



This isn't a role for someone who manages a calendar and waits for instructions. This is a role for someone who sees what needs to happen before they're asked, and makes it happen before anyone notices it was a problem.



We're growing fast. The Founder is deep in client delivery, sales, expansion, and content simultaneously. To keep scaling without losing quality, we need one person who owns the operational and administrative infrastructure around him.

That's you. If you're ready.



***About Growth Dinners***



Growth Dinners operates and runs curated executive dinner experiences for B2B companies across multiple U.S. cities. Our clients are sponsors and hosts who use our dinners to access hard-to-reach buyers, build relationships with C-suite prospects, and accelerate pipeline in a room where business actually gets done.



We're a lean, founder-led operation with high standards, fast decisions, and a bias toward action. Every hire matters here.

What You'll Own



***Event & Dinner Operations***



Coordinate logistics for B2B executive dinners end-to-end — venues, RSVPs, run-of-show, day-of execution, and post-event follow-up

Build and maintain event timelines, checklists, and SOPs that reduce friction and prevent anything from falling through the cracks

Manage contractor communication and vendor coordination before and after each event

Track event deliverables and ensure all client commitments are met on schedule



*** Founder Calendar & Inbox***



Own the Founder's calendar — proactively protect high-output time, schedule with intent, and eliminate scheduling back-and-forth

Triage and manage inbox communications — draft responses, flag priorities, and handle routine correspondence independently

Prepare the Founder for every meeting with relevant context, agenda, and background

Follow up on open loops so nothing stalls due to a missed reply

Client Communications & Follow-Up

Draft and manage client-facing communications with professionalism and attention to brand voice

Track client deliverables, deadlines, and milestones across active engagements

Coordinate onboarding touchpoints for new dinner sponsors and ensure a smooth client experience from contract to event day

Send post-event summaries, attribution documentation, and follow-up sequences on behalf of the Founder



*** CRM & Pipeline Management***



Keep the CRM clean, updated, and actionable — log activity, update deal stages, and flag stale contacts

Manage outreach sequences and ensure follow-ups are sent on schedule

Build and maintain contact lists for prospective dinner guests and event sponsors

Surface weekly pipeline reports and flag anything that needs the Founder's attention



** Who You Are**



You're the kind of person who finishes something before being asked twice. You think in systems. You write with clarity. And you take ownership like it's your business — because in many ways, it will be.

You have 3–4 years of experience in an Executive Assistant, Operations Coordinator, or similar role — ideally in a fast-moving startup, agency, or events environment

You're a strong writer who can draft
s, client communications, and follow-ups that sound like a human wrote them.

Your AI is fluent and up to speed on how to use the latest AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, etc. to maximize efficiency and productivity.

You understand basic automation tools like Zapier and 
 or are excited to jump in and to learn them on your first day.

You have a working knowledge of CRM tools & SOPs databases (HubSpot, Notion, Airtable, or similar) and don't need to be trained from zero.

You're proactive — you don't wait to be told the same thing twice

You communicate ahead of problems, not after

You've read Buy Back Your Time by Dan Martell or you're ready to — because that's the operating system we run on

You thrive in a remote environment and take ownership of your own structure and accountability



***What Success Looks Like in 30 Days***



The Founder's inbox and calendar are under control — no missed follow-ups, no double-bookings, no surprise deadlines

Every active dinner event has a documented run-of-show and client communication log

The CRM is clean, current, and being used to drive follow-up — not just store data

You've proactively identified and solved at least two recurring operational problems without being asked



***Our Hiring Process***

We move fast and we value your time. Here's exactly what to expect:

1. Submit a 1-minute Loom video answering: Why are you the right fit for this role? What's a system or process you built that made someone's work life meaningfully easier?

2. 15-minute intro call — quick vibe check on expectations and fit

3. Short skills assessment — we'll send you a realistic task (paid) to see how you think and execute

4. Final interview with the Founder

5. Offer — if we're both excited, we move immediately

Applications without a Loom video will not be reviewed. 

Generic videos that don't address the question above will also not be reviewed. 

This role is remote, part-time 10 hours per week with ability to grow into full-time role.

We work together on a paid project, then move to part-time, and finally full-time after you've demonstrated you're a fit.

To apply, send your Loom video + resume to 
 with subject line: "EA Application — [Your Name]"

title: Copywriter & Creative Strategist
description:
Overview

We are seeking a "pioneer"—a proactive, high-energy Copywriter & Creative Strategist to join an experimental, high-conviction project for 2026. This is not a traditional retainer-based role; it is a pure commission-based affiliate model designed to empower our creators and scale our brands through organic social lift. 

In this role, you will act as the "owner" of your own AI-driven Instagram presence, providing the creative vision and briefs while our elite internal production studio handles the AI generation for you—free of charge.



Work Structure & Compensation

Type: Part-time / Commission-based (Affiliate).

Compensation: 10%–50% commission per sale (e.g., a 50% commission is $50 on a $100 product).

Transparency: Commission rates are tiered and will change over time as the project scales.

Growth Opportunity: The highest-performing candidates will be fast-tracked into full-time retainer roles with the company.

Work Requirement: You may have a concurrent full-time job; however, all candidates must sign an NDA



Key Responsibilities

Creative Briefing: Develop winning formats such as AI-driven podcasts, TED talks, or street interviews to sell products to a health-conscious 45+ demographic.

Production Partnership: Brief our internal AI production studio to create high-fidelity, realistic B-roll and AI-influencer content.

Account Management: Run and grow your own Instagram accounts dedicated to promoting the brand.

Performance Testing: Experiment with different formats and AI personas to find the highest-converting proof of concept.

Scaling Strategy: Identify "winner" content and whitelist these assets with our paid ads to "put gasoline on the fire" and scale results.

Strategic Iteration: Use organic performance data to refine hooks, calls-to-action (CTAs), and storytelling strategies. 



Required Skills & Experience

Direct Response Copywriting: Proven ability to write clear, compelling, and high-converting scripts and briefs focused on driving immediate action.

Strategic Thinking: Deep understanding of marketing fundamentals and consumer psychology (e.g., Eugene Schwartz’s Breakthrough Advertising) to create "thumb-stopping" social media content.

AI Awareness: Comfortable working within AI-driven workflows; if not currently comfortable, you must be highly willing to learn and adapt quickly.

Market Knowledge: Understanding of the "TikTok Shop" model and how to apply it to
 and Instagram.

Mindset: A true team player who is communicative, proactive, and maintains a "team-first" attitude while operating with a pioneer spirit.



Why Join Us?

Resource Support: We will work to provide as many resources as possible to ensure your success, including free access to our world-class production studio.

Faceless Commission: A unique, faceless way to earn significant commissions by running your own AI influencer pages.

Direct Impact: We operate on a "real relationship" basis—we don’t make money unless you make money.



To Apply

Please submit your portfolio or examples of previous creative strategies. Include a short Loom video (2–3 minutes) introducing yourself and explaining why you are the right person to pioneer this AI-affiliate model.

title: Property Operations Coordinator
description:
Remote Property Operations Coordinator (AU Short-Term Rentals)

Philippines-based | Full-time | AEST-aligned



Join One of Australia’s Largest Short-Term Rental Businesses



This is a rare long-term opportunity to work directly with one of Australia’s most established short-term rental operators — supporting a growing portfolio of up to 800 properties across NSW.



Staff Connect Australia is building a high-performing offshore team, and we’re looking for a proactive, detail-driven Property Operations Coordinator to become one of our foundational hires. If you’re sharp, confident on calls, and love problem-solving in a fast-paced environment, this role has serious growth potential.



You’ll be right at the heart of daily operations — handling bookings, guest and owner communications, issue resolution, and admin support — with exposure to every part of Australian short-term rental management.



What You’ll Be Doing



This is a hands-on, dynamic role. Your responsibilities will include:

- Guest & Owner Support

- Handle inbound and outbound calls with guests and property owners via Aircall

- Respond to enquiries and issues via Airbnb, 
, direct bookings

- Resolve problems efficiently and follow through to completion





Operations & Coordination

- Monitor bookings, availability, and calendars using Homhero, RMS, and other systems

- Coordinate cleaning schedules and ensure properties are guest-ready

- Assign and follow up maintenance tasks with trades or contractors

- Track cleans, maintenance, guest issues, and owner communications



Admin, Reporting & Growth

- Manage booking calendars, pricing, and blackout dates

- Assist with onboarding new properties and system setup

- Support compliance documentation (smoke alarms, council registration, etc.)

- Prepare weekly/monthly reports using spreadsheets or dashboards

- Help draft and improve SOPs, internal docs, and guest manuals

- Identify workflow gaps and suggest smarter processes or automations



As the business grows, your role will grow with it — including pricing, owner reporting, SOP ownership, and broader operational oversight.



Who We’re Looking For



You’re someone who:

- Is confident on the phone and professional with both guests and property owners

- Thrives in fast-paced, customer-facing environments

- Is organised, proactive, and solutions-focused

- Can juggle multiple tools and tasks without being micromanaged

- Takes ownership and actually cares about doing things properly



Skills & Experience



Required:

- Excellent spoken & written English

- Stable internet connection

- Confident handling phone calls

- Experience (or fast learning ability) with Aircall, Homhero

- Familiarity with Airbnb, 
, and direct booking sites

- Strong organisational & multitasking skills

- Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook)



Preferred (but not mandatory)



- 2+ years in short-term rentals, property management, or admin support

- Experience working with Australian clients

- Exposure to cleaning teams or remote contractors



We’re open to candidates with less experience if you show initiative, strong communication skills, and the right mindset.



Tools You’ll Use

Core Systems:

- Homhero, RMS, Breezeway, Orana

- Comms & Support:

- Aircall, Slack, Zoom, MS Teams, Google Meet, WhatsApp

- Automation & Docs:

- ChatGPT, Zapier, Google Docs, Canva, Microsoft Office, WordPress



Bonus Tools:

- PriceLabs or other pricing platforms





What You’ll Get:



- Full-time role – 40 hrs/week (AEST-aligned)

- Structured roster – 7-day operation with rotating shifts

- You’ll work one or both weekend days

- Always two consecutive days off each week

- Paid training & onboarding

- Direct mentorship from leadership

- Long-term career growth in a scaling business

- Exposure to end-to-end Australian STR operations



A role where initiative, ownership, and ideas are genuinely valued

title: Interactive TikTok LIVE Game Developer (TikTok API / Stream Integration)
description:
???? Job Description:



We are building interactive games for TikTok LIVE streamers that increase engagement, viewer retention, and gift conversions.



We are looking for an experienced developer who can build real-time interactive games that connect to TikTok LIVE events (gifts, likes, comments, follows, etc.).



This will be part of a larger platform (future SaaS), so we are looking for someone reliable for long-term work.



???? What You’ll Be Building:

Interactive LIVE games (examples):

Lucky Wheel (spin triggered by gifts)

Runner / obstacle game (chat or gifts control movement)

Drop/avalanche-style games (real-time chaos based on engagement)

Real-time event handling (gifts, likes, follows, chat)

Clean UI that streamers can display on OBS / TikTok Studio

Config system (users can customize thresholds, triggers, rewards)

?? Requirements:

Strong experience with:

JavaScript / TypeScript OR Python

WebSockets / real-time systems

Experience working with:

TikTok LIVE APIs (or unofficial libraries like TikTok Live Connector)

OBS overlays / browser sources

Understanding of:

Event-driven systems

Low-latency interaction (important for LIVE)

Bonus:

Experience with tools like TikFinity, Streamlabs, or similar

Game dev basics (2D logic, animations)

???? Payment:

Pay per game OR monthly (depending on experience)

Budget: Open (looking for quality + long-term partner)

title: Client Success Manager (Remote)
description:
KYRI MEDIA — Client Success Manager Remote • Full-Time 

Apply here: 
 





About Kyri Media Kyri Media is a remote real estate video marketing agency. We help US-based real estate agents grow their brand and generate leads through short-form video content and social media management. Our delivery team operates from the Philippines, our clients are across the United States, and our standard is simple: deliver results that agents can point to. We move fast, we own our work, and we don't make excuses.



The Role The Client Success Manager owns the client relationship from the 
nt someone signs to the 
nt they renew — and everything in between. Your job is not to manage tasks. Your job is to keep clients engaged, believing in the system, and consistently showing up to produce content. You are the early warning system: if a client is going quiet, slowing down, or losing confidence, you catch it before they do and you fix it before it becomes a problem.



This role requires someone with genuine patience and care — not scripted empathy, but the real kind that keeps clients motivated through slow weeks and self-doubt. You will also need the conviction to hold the line when a client pushes back on the process, and the relationship-building instinct to make them feel heard while still keeping them accountable.



What You Own You are responsible for running every onboarding call, setting clear expectations, and booking the first recording session before hanging up. Every client sees their numbers weekly and monthly — you send the report before they think to ask. It is your job to keep clients showing up to film, to flag content quality issues to the team immediately, and to escalate any client concern directly to the founder the 
nt it surfaces.



You will actively monitor engagement signals to detect churn risk early, identify opportunities to expand client results, and drive renewals through the belief you build month by month. Retention is not a metric you report on — it is something you engineer. That is on you.



Who You Are



You are organised to a degree most people aren't. You manage multiple clients, open threads, and time-sensitive follow-ups without dropping a single item. You respect deadlines because you understand that a missed touchpoint is a client that starts drifting.



You have a background in customer support or account management, and you understand the social media landscape well enough to speak credibly about content performance with real estate agents who are new to the game. You know what good short-form content looks like, and you are comfortable having those conversations.



You have enough sales experience to recognise when a client is at risk and to reframe the value of the service before they start questioning it. You know how to turn a hesitant renewal into an easy yes — not through pressure, but through genuine results and relationship.



You are coachable. You do not treat feedback as criticism. You treat it as information, and you act on it fast. You are obsessed with getting better at your craft — whether that is how you run an onboarding call, how you write a follow-up message, or how quickly you spot a client losing 
ntum.



You pay close attention to detail — in the reports you send, the notes you keep, and the language you use with clients. Small things matter here.



Requirements You have fluent written and spoken English, and you are confident running structured Zoom calls where clients leave feeling informed and excited. You have experience in a client-facing or account management role and you are comfortable working across GHL (GoHighLevel), ClickUp, and Google Workspace. You manage your time independently across multiple priorities without needing to be chased, and you are available to align with US time zones for client calls.



What We Offer This is a fully remote, permanent position with direct access to the founder — no layers of management and no politics. Your impact is measurable in real time: you will see client results week by week. The team operates with a low-ego, high-execution culture where the people who take ownership grow with the business.





Compensation This role is open to applicants worldwide, including the United States, South Africa, the Philippines, Europe, and beyond. Compensation ranges from $500 to $5,000 per month depending on your experience level and location. In addition to your base, you will earn a 5% commission on every client renewal you own. We will discuss the specific range that applies to your profile during the interview process.



Apply here:

s, and files

• Identify and escalate security i

title: English Tutor
description:
Job Title: Academic Tutor (English)



Location: Remote (Global)



Type: Part-Time



Reports To: Director of Academic Programs



About the Role:

Telos Prep is seeking an ethical, professional, and reliable English Tutor to join our remote team. We operate on a specialized team-based model, where students receive comprehensive support from a network of Head Counselors, Essay Editors, and Subject Tutors. As a tutor, you will provide critical subject-specific support to help our students strengthen their academic foundations.



What You'll Do:



- Subject-Specific Tutoring: Provide expert tutoring in English Literature and Language (including AP/Honors levels), focusing on building student confidence in reading comprehension, analytical writing, and academic excellence.

- Student-Centered Instruction: Emphasize clear explanations and a Socratic, student-participation approach to learning rather than simple lecturing.

- Team Collaboration: Work seamlessly within our team-based model, aligning your academic support with the broader strategic goals set by the student's Head Counselor.

- Prompt Communication: Maintain professional, timely communication to support students and families. Respond to client and internal communications promptly, typically within 24 business hours.



Who You Are:



- Educational Background: A Bachelor's degree in English, Literature, Education, or a closely related field is required.

- Tutoring Experience: Proven experience tutoring high school students in English, with a strong preference for candidates familiar with the AP English Language and AP English Literature curricula.

- Tech-Savvy: Comfortable working in a remote, AI-enhanced environment where technology is used to serve the relationship and optimize operations.



Schedule & Work Environment:



- Remote Setup: This role is structured as remote (Global).

- Flexible Hours: Work hours are flexible within reason, provided all deliverables are completed on time and client communication remains timely and professional.

- Availability: Must be available during after-school hours (3:00 PM to 10:00 PM U.S. Time) and on weekends. *(Note: Please consider the time zone difference from your local region when evaluating these hours).*



What We Offer:



- Pay: $10/hour

- Resources: Telos will provide the necessary software subscriptions and tools required for the role.

title: Project Manager
description:
Job Title: Project Manager

Location: Remote (Philippines-based)

Type: Contractual

Reports To: Principal & Founder (with close collaboration with the COO/Head of Operations)



About the Role:

Telos Prep is seeking an ethical, professional, and reliable Project Manager to join our team. We operate on a highly specialized "Team-Based Model," meaning our students are supported not just by one counselor, but by a network of Head Counselors, Essay Editors, Tutors, and Purpose Project Mentors. In this role, you will be the operational glue holding this model together, ensuring our academic advising and project mentorship programs run smoothly. You will serve as the central hub for our team, requiring strong leadership skills to manage workflows and coordinate our remote staff.



What You'll Do:



- Team Coordination & Leadership: Act as the primary point of contact and operational leader for our cross-functional team. Support Head Counselors by ensuring that Essay Editors, Tutors, and Purpose Project Mentors are aligned and executing on their deliverables.

- Progress Tracking: Track student progress and application deadlines across the counseling team, as well as manage and track internal projects being executed by the Telos team.

- Scheduling & Logistics: Coordinate and manage complex schedules between students, Head Counselors, Editors, Tutors, and internal stakeholders.

- Process Improvement: Assist with the development and refinement of internal tracking tools and standard operating procedures (SOPs) within Notion to boost team efficiency.

- Prompt Communication: Respond to client and internal communications promptly, ensuring our standard of a 24-business-hour turnaround is met.



Who You Are:



- Team Management Experience: Proven experience leading, coordinating, or managing teams in a similar remote, agency, or educational setting. Comfortable overseeing workflows for multiple stakeholders and ensuring team accountability.

- Project Management: Strong background in tracking deliverables, managing timelines, and handling logistics for multiple ongoing projects simultaneously.

- Tech-Savvy Operations: Experience working in an "AI-Enhanced" environment. Proficiency in project management databases (e.g. Notion) is highly preferred.



Schedule & Work Environment:



- Fully Remote: This role allows you to work primarily from home.

- Flexible Hours: Work hours are flexible within reason, provided all deliverables are completed on time, team oversight is maintained, and client communication remains timely and professional.

- Availability: Must be available during after-school hours (3:00 PM to 10:00 PM Pacific Time) and on weekends. (Note: 3:00 PM to 10:00 PM Pacific usually translates to 6:00 AM to 1:00 PM or 7:00 AM to 2:00 PM PHT, depending on US Daylight Saving Time).



What We Offer:

- Pay: $900/month

- Resources: Telos will provide the necessary software subscriptions and tools required for the role.

title: Professional Website Builder Shopify/Wordpress
description:
We are an Australian based branding and apparel company looking for a Shopify or Wordpress expert to assist with building a Shopify store for a short term project.



This is a project based role, however if the work is done well there may be ongoing opportunities for future projects.



We are looking for someone who is confident building a store from the ground up and can work efficiently without constant supervision.



Scope of Work



The project will involve:



• Setting up a Shopify/Wordpress store from scratch

• Creating Installing and configuring a Shopify/Wordpress theme

• Creating collections and navigation menus

• Uploading products with variants (Price, sizes and colours)

• Uploading product images and descriptions

• Setting up policies and basic store settings

• Ensuring the store layout is clean and professional

• Making sure everything is organised and easy to manage



Some products will include multiple variants, so experience working with Price, size and colour variants is essential.



Requirements



• Strong experience building Shopify/Wordpress Sites

• Confident working with product variants

• Good understanding of store layout and organisation

• Attention to detail

• Ability to follow instructions

• Fast and reliable communication

• Able to complete work within agreed timeframe

• Experience working with apparel products

• Experience building Shopify/Wordpress stores for clothing brands is a MUST

• Basic graphic design skills

• Experience optimising images for web use



Project Details



• Short term project

• Estimated duration: 1 to 3 weeks

• Potential for ongoing work if successful

• Must be able to communicate clearly and provide progress updates



To Apply



Please include:



• Examples of Shopify/Wordpress stores you have built

• A brief description of your role in those projects

• Your estimated timeframe to complete a store build

• Your hourly rate or project rate



Important:

Start your application with the word “STORE” so we know you have read this job description properly.



Applicants who have built Sites for custom apparel companies encouraged to apply
